$comment: DRAFT fragments to merge into schema/sofabuffers-schema-v1.json to add the `map` type. This is NOT a standalone schema - each numbered key below says exactly where its fragment slots into the existing file. Companion to docs/plans/maps.md. draft-07, closed objects, matching the existing style.

1_add_map_to_field_type_enum.$comment: At #/$defs/field/properties/type/enum -- append the string "map" to the existing list (u8..union).

2_add_map_to_arrayItems_type_enum.$comment: At #/$defs/arrayItems/properties/type/enum -- append "map" so a map is also a valid array element and map value.

3_new_def_mapKey.$comment: Add as a new entry under the top-level #/$defs (next to `enum`, `bitfield`, `struct`, `union`). Restricts a map key to hashable/comparable leaves; float/blob/composite keys are rejected structurally and named by the `mapKeyType` custom keyword.

mapKey.description: Map key element. Keys are restricted to hashable/comparable leaf types so every target lowers them onto a native map key.

type.description: Key element type. Excludes fp32/fp64 (equality/NaN hazard), blob, struct, union, array, map.

maxlen.description: Maximum byte length of a string key. Only valid when type is string; required by fixed-storage targets so the key slot can be pre-sized.

enum.description: Member definitions when type is enum.

$ref.description: Reference to a predefined enum in $defs.

4_field_map_branch.$comment: Append this if/then to the `allOf` array inside #/$defs/field (the same list that holds the u8/string/array/struct/union branches). Closed to key/value/count + shared metadata.

count.description: Capacity: maximum entry count. Optional, but required by fixed-storage targets (C always; C++ c-cpp / Rust no_std unless allow_dynamic) so the entry array can be pre-sized. The wire may carry 0..count entries; never appears on the wire. Also governs the receiver-side decode cap (max_dyn_map_entries).

5_field_reverse_guards.$comment: Also append these to #/$defs/field/allOf. Defensive twins of the per-type additionalProperties:false closures, giving a clear `must be type: map` error (the field object itself is additionalProperties:true).

6_arrayItems_map_support.$comment: Lets a map be an array element (array-of-map) and a map value (map-valued map). (a) add these two properties to #/$defs/arrayItems/properties; (b) append the if/then guards to #/$defs/arrayItems/allOf. arrayItems already carries `count` (the element/dimension capacity), which for a map element is its entry capacity - no new key needed there.

7_custom_keyword_mapKeyType.$comment: New custom validation keyword to add to the reference validator (schema/README.md Validation + ARCHITECTURE 5), alongside uniqueIds/defaultMatchesEnum/etc. The `enum` in mapKey rejects a bad key type structurally; this keyword produces the located, human error naming the field.
7_custom_keyword_mapKeyType.spec: mapKeyType (attached to #/$defs/mapKey): the key element `type` MUST be one of u8,u16,u32,u64,i8,i16,i32,i64,boolean,string,enum. On violation, fail closed with the offending field's location (message/struct path + field name), like the other custom keywords. Runs after $ref resolution so a {$ref} enum key is checked as an enum.
